chapter_name: Dad and The Cat and The Tree

questions.question_text: Why was Dad sure he wouldn't fall? Answer in the context of Dad and The Cat and The Tree.
questions.question_html: Why was Dad sure he wouldn’t fall? Answer in the context of Dad and The Cat and The Tree.

solutions.text: Dad felt assured that he wouldn't fall as he considered himself to be a climber and thought that climbing trees was a child's play.
solutions.html: Dad felt assured that he wouldn’t fall as he considered himself to be a climber and thought that climbing trees was a child’s play.

questions.question_text: Which phrase in the poem expresses Dad's self-confidence best? [Dad and The Cat and The Tree]
questions.question_html: Which phrase in the poem expresses Dad’s self-confidence best? [Dad and The Cat and The Tree]

solutions.text: The phrase 'Child's play, this is!' expresses Dad's self-confidence.
solutions.html: The phrase 'Child’s play, this is!' expresses Dad’s self-confidence.

questions.question_text: Describe Plan A and its consequences. Answer in the context of Dad and The Cat and The Tree.
questions.question_html: Describe Plan A and its consequences. Answer in the context of Dad and The Cat and The Tree.

solutions.text: Plan A was to use the ladder to climb up the tree and carry down the cat, but the ladder slipped and Dad fell into the flower bed.
solutions.html: Plan A was to use the ladder to climb up the tree and carry down the cat, but the ladder slipped and Dad fell into the flower bed.

questions.question_text: Plan C was a success. What went wrong then? Answer in the content of Dad and The Cat and The Tree.
questions.question_html: Plan C was a success. What went wrong then? Answer in the content of Dad and The Cat and The Tree.

solutions.text: Plan C was a success as Dad successfully jumped onto the tree from the top of the garden wall, but he landed right on the cat. The cat gave a yell and sprang down onto the ground but poor old Dad was left stuck on the tree.
solutions.html: Plan C was a success as Dad successfully jumped onto the tree from the top of the garden wall, but he landed right on the cat. The cat gave a yell and sprang down onto the ground but poor old Dad was left stuck on the tree.

questions.question_text: The cat was very happy to be on the ground. Pick out the phrase used to express this idea. [Dad and The Cat and The Tree]
questions.question_html: The cat was very happy to be on the ground. Pick out the phrase used to express this idea. [Dad and The Cat and The Tree]

solutions.text: The phrase used to express this idea is 'Pleased as Punch to be'.
solutions.html: The phrase used to express this idea is 'Pleased as Punch to be'.

questions.question_text: Describe the Cat and Dad situation in the beginning and at the end of the poem.
questions.question_html: Describe the Cat and Dad situation in the beginning and at the end of the poem.

solutions.text: In the beginning of the poem, the cat was stuck on the tree and Dad was trying his plans to bring it down but at the end of the poem the cat was on the ground, safe and sound, and Dad was left stuck on the tree.
solutions.html: In the beginning of the poem, the cat was stuck on the tree and Dad was trying his plans to bring it down but at the end of the poem the cat was on the ground, safe and sound, and Dad was left stuck on the tree.

questions.question_text:
Why and when did Dad say each of the following?
Fall?
Never mind
Funny joke
Rubbish
questions.question_html:
Why and when did Dad say each of the following?

  1. Fall?
  2. Never mind
  3. Funny joke
  4. Rubbish

solutions.text:
When his wife warned him against falling, a father with a great surprise said "fall?"
Having fallen to the ground, the father did not give up and brushing the dirt from his hair and clothes said, "never mind."
When his wife again warned him against falling while he was executing Plan B, he found his wife's warnings to be a "funny joke."
After the second fall, when his wife intervened saying that he might break his neck, he again dismissed her concern by saying, "rubbish."
solutions.html:
1. When his wife warned him against falling, a father with a great surprise said "fall?"
  2. Having fallen to the ground, the father did not give up and brushing the dirt from his hair and clothes said, "never mind."
  3. When his wife again warned him against falling while he was executing Plan B, he found his wife's warnings to be a "funny joke."
  4. After the second fall, when his wife intervened saying that he might break his neck, he again dismissed her concern by saying, "rubbish."

questions.question_text: Do you find the poem humorous? Read aloud lines which make you laugh. Answer in the context of Dad and The Cat and The Tree.
questions.question_html: Do you find the poem humorous? Read aloud lines which make you laugh. Answer in the context of Dad and The Cat and The Tree.

solutions.text: Yes, the poem is humorous. It draws out funny situations. Dad comes across with while trying to climb a tree, to rescue a cat. Moreover, Dad's overconfidence (about being able to climb the tree very easily as if it were a child's play adds humour to the poem) and the scepticism of Mum adds humour that reaches its height when at the end Dad remains stuck in the tree while the Cat jumps down safely on its own.
solutions.html: Yes, the poem is humorous. It draws out funny situations. Dad comes across with while trying to climb a tree, to rescue a cat. Moreover, Dad's overconfidence (about being able to climb the tree very easily as if it were a child's play adds humour to the poem) and the scepticism of Mum adds humour that reaches its height when at the end Dad remains stuck in the tree while the Cat jumps down safely on its own.
